definition:
1. to breathe again, take breath , (Iliad by Homer), etc.: to recover from  a thing, with genitive, (Iliad by Homer), etc.; so, ἔκ τινος (Herdotus Historicus):—; absolute to revive , (Xenophon Historicus); and in this sense (Homer) uses ἄμπνυτο, ἀμπνύνθη. 
2. to draw breath, breathe , (Pindar) 
3. to breathe forth, send forth , καπνόν (Pindar) (ML)
